2003-04-01.title: Conflicting Reports on North Korea Missile Test Add to Tensions
2003-04-01.abstract: Conflicting reports about whether North Korea test-fired a short-range missile off its east coast brought new tensions to the region today.

2003-04-01.title: Jordan Arrests Iraqis in Plot to Poison U.S. Troops' Water
2003-04-01.abstract: The Jordanian authorities have arrested a handful of Iraqi agents in connection with a botched plot to poison the water supply that serves American troops, officials said today.

2003-04-01.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-04-01.abstract: SRI LANKA: Rebel Boat Sunk In Battle ; JAPAN: Farm Minister Quits Over Scandal;.

2003-04-01.title: Bush renews vow to 'free' Iraqi people
2003-04-01.abstract: WASHINGTON:—  President George W. Bush told a gathering of Coast Guard officers Monday that coalition forces in Iraq were "day by day moving closer to Baghdad."

2003-04-01.title: A NATION AT WAR: IN THE FIELD | 24th MARINE EXPEDITIONARY UNIT; Troops Heading Home Are Redirected to the Gulf
2003-04-01.abstract: Marine Expeditionary Unit, due to go home after six-month tour abroad, is redeployed to Iraq; 2,300 member unit specializes in house-to-house combat, as well as peacekeeping and aid missions (M)

2003-04-02.title: Virus Imperils Commerce And Economy In Asian Hub
2003-04-02.abstract: Hong Kong's economic setbacks in years since transition back to China are dwarfed by explosive outbreak of severe respiratory disease that is spreading panic and undermining confidence in future of one of world's most dynamic cities; government appeals for calm (M)

2003-04-02.title: Commandos Rescue Soldier; She Was Held Since Ambush
2003-04-02.abstract: Special Operations forces rescue Pfc Jessica Lynch, 19, from hospital in Nasirya, Iraq, where she was held captive since March 23, when her army maintenance unit took wrong turn and was attacked; others in 15-member unit are listed as missing, captured or confirmed dead; secret raid is carried out after US intelligence learns of whereabouts (M)

2003-04-02.title: Daughter of the Revolution Fights the Veil
2003-04-02.abstract: Zahra Eshraghi, granddaughter of Ayatollah Khomeini, sister-in-law of Pres Mohammad Khatami and wife of head of reformist wing of Iran's parliament, has enormous standing in Iranian society, and uses it to state frankly her hatred of chador, black veil forced on women since her grandfather's revolution; interview; photo; Eshraghi, 39, is herself government official who has fought colleagues at Interior Ministry, where she promotes women's issues; her candor also reflects changes in Iran, where revolution's values have given way to intense, even dangerous debate about religion's place in politics and society (M)

2003-04-02.title: Briefly Noted
2003-04-02.abstract: EGYPT TO EXPEL IRAQI ENVOY Egypt has decided to expel a senior Iraqi diplomat, giving him a week to leave the country. An Arab diplomat who monitors Iraqi affairs in Cairo said the order was served Tuesday morning.

2003-04-02.title: An Outfit's Wise Guy Sees the Lighter Side of Even the Somber Moments
2003-04-02.abstract: There is one in the back row of every classroom, in every nest of office cubicles, at the end of the bar at every happy hour. And, as it turns out, there is one in a tent of men exhausted by the preparations for war, their aching feet sliding into cool sleeping bags.    ''Aw, look at all the girls going to sleep,'' came the voice from a makeshift table in a corner of the tent, the small patch of hair on the big head dulled with dust. ''It's like a slumber party.'' He shuffled a deck of cards between his thick fingers and continued, in a lilting falsetto: ''Hey, did that guy ever call you? Really? Wow! In just two days?'' The men tried to ignore him.    It was mid-March in Kuwait, and the war had not yet begun. Unlike most of the other marines in the tent, the Deuce was not tired. The Deuce was playing cards, and it was the Deuce's deal.

2003-04-03.title: G.I.'s Pry Iraqis Loose And Surge Over River
2003-04-03.abstract: Army's Third Infantry Division clears last geographic obstacle to Baghdad, 20 miles away, after it seizes damaged bridge over Euphrates River following intense three-hour battle against more than company of dug-in Iraqi soldiers; thousands of American soldiers surge across bridge in tanks, Bradleys, mobile artillery batteries and array of other armored vehicles; there are no immediate reports of American casualties; battle for bridge described; Iraqi tactics that proved efective against forces encircling Najaf, Nasiriya and Samawa--sniping, ambushes, taxi bomb--could do little to slow division's armored forces; photo (M)

2003-04-03.title: South Korea Agrees to Send Troops to Iraq
2003-04-03.abstract: South Korean Parliament approves plan to send 700 soldiers to Iraq to take part in reconstruction effort; decision follows forceful plea by new Pres Roh Moo Hyun (S)

2003-04-03.title: Japanese Hail 'The Mikado,' Long-Banned Imperial Spoof
2003-04-03.abstract: The Mikado, Gilbert and Sullivan operetta that spoofs Japanese imperial family, is being presented in Chichibu, in first production for mainstream Japanese audience; it has been filling 1,000-seat theater; one of lead roles is being played by daughter of head priest of Chichibu; photo; map (M)

2003-04-03.title: Virus Is Yet Another Blow to Battered Airlines As Companies Limit Employees' Trips to Asia
2003-04-03.abstract: Rapid spread of deadly respiratory disease SARS is cutting into already battered airline industry, as carriers around world cancel empty flights, lay off workers, screen passengers for symptoms and deal with crews that refuse to fly to some Asian cities; chart (M)

2003-04-03.title: Arab TV Curtails Coverage After Move by Iraq
2003-04-03.abstract: Arab television network Al Jazeera says Iraq has barred its two correspondents in Baghdad from reporting, and that it will respond by suspending coverage of all its correspondents accredited by Iraq; Iraqi move remains hard to decipher (S)

2003-04-04.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-04-04.abstract: CHECHNYA: Deadly Bus Blast; IRELAND: Plane Diverted; ITALY: CrÈChe Of Cocaine;.

2003-04-04.title: Having Seized Baghdad Airport, Army Ponders Next Move
2003-04-04.abstract: Thousands of soldiers poured into the Baghdad airport today without a clear sense of what military mission lies ahead.

2003-04-04.title: Chirac Apologizes for Vandalized Graves
2003-04-04.abstract: Pres Jacques Chirac of France writes letter of apology to Queen Elizabeth over desecration of graves of British soldiers from World War I at cemetery in France; graves bore graffiti denouncing war in Iraq (M)

2003-04-04.title: British Court Orders Extradition in French Bombing Case
2003-04-04.abstract: A British court ordered today the extradition of an Algerian man accused of participation in a series of bombings in the Paris subway and commuter train system in 1995.

2003-04-04.title: Hoping to Confuse Iraqis and Spread Panic, American Commandos Prepare to Enter Baghdad
2003-04-04.abstract: American commando teams are moving into locations around Baghdad in preparation for final push into city; teams, which are typically fewer than 12 men, are setting up stations from which they can locate targets inside city, gather information on Iraqi troop movements and coordinate Iraqi opposition groups' actions; goal is to neutralize Baath Party militias and Saddam Fedayeen, who have been mounting guerrilla attacks on American and British forces in south; teams will also search for Iraqi leaders, attempting to either capture them or pinpoint locations for American warplanes; Special Forces soldiers hope to spread havoc inside Iraqi lines and panic to military forces and political leaders (M)

2003-04-05.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: New Commons Leader
2003-04-05.abstract: Labor Party head John Reid is appointed leader of House of Commons to replace Robin Cook, who resigned because of Britain's decision to enter Iraq war without UN approval; photo (S)

2003-04-05.title: African Mission to Investigate Tensions in Zimbabwe
2003-04-05.abstract: Southern African Development Community forms task force to probe intensifying political tensions that threaten to push Zimbabwe toward further unrest and economic ruin; at issue is dispute between Pres Robert Mugabe and political opponents who charge his authoritarian rule has undermined democracy and devastated economy; Zimbabweans fleeing hunger and unemployment are becoming drain on South Africa; Western governments are pressing South African Pres Thabo Mbeki to take stronger stand against Mugabe's human-rights violations (M)

2003-04-05.title: Bush and Blair Will Meet In Belfast Early Next Week On Iraq, Mideast and Ulster
2003-04-05.abstract: Pres Bush will meet with Prime Min Tony Blair of Britain in Belfast beginning April 6 to discuss war in Iraq and peace efforts in Northern Ireland and Israel; meeting will focus on when to declare existence of new Iraqi government, event that American officials now say could come even before government of Saddam Hussein is toppled or Baghdad is secured; Bush meets at White House with dozen Iraqi exiles, who speak in unified voice of their support for Bush and war to overthrow Hussein, whom they accuse of human rights abuses and atrocities; photo (M)

2003-04-05.title: 10 Americans and 400 Kurds, With Help From U.S. Bombs, Stave Off Iraqis for Now
2003-04-05.abstract: Ten-member American Special Operation team and 400 Kurds take on as many as 2,000 Iraqi soldiers outside Khazir with help of American warplanes in heaviest fighting in northern Iraq since war began; Iraqi soldiers led by three tanks threaten to take back strategic bridge leading to Mosul from which they pulled back on April 3; US soldiers' primary function is to guide airstrikes from ground, which removes them from fighting at close quarters; photo (M)

2003-04-05.title: F-16's Pick Off Iraqi Tanks In 'Kill Box' Near Baghdad
2003-04-05.abstract: Air Force captain describes how he and pilot of another F-16CG destroyed six Iraqi tanks in 'kill box' just north of Baghdad; kill boxes are areas of several square miles where pilots may fire at will once they positively identify target and make sure that attack will not kill civilians or damage nearby buildings like schools, mosques or hospitals; are used to prevent casualties among allied forces on ground or accidential clashes with allied planes in air (M)

2003-04-06.title: Italian Leader Faces Dissent Over Control Of the Media
2003-04-06.abstract: Lower house of Italy's Parliament passes fiercely contested bill that opposition critics say is intended to expand Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi's television interests, but opposition garners enough votes to amend bill to severely limit its scope and reach; Berlusconi and his supporters seem confident that bill will be readjusted in May by Senate (M)

2003-04-06.title: Lightning Raid in Baghdad, Commando Operations and Postwar Planning
2003-04-06.abstract: Overview of day's events in Iraq war (S)

2003-04-06.title: U.S. Tanks Make Quick Strike Into Baghdad
2003-04-06.abstract: Members of armored battalion of Army's Third Infantry Division describe looping drive through center of Baghdad, blistering gantlet of death and destruction that they say engulfed civilians as well as Iraqi fighters; Maj Gen Buford C Blount III says there was no attempt at occupation, simply a show of force to answer reports on Iraqi television that Americans had become bogged down in fighting around city's international airport and farther south; photo; map (M)

2003-04-06.title: Kurdish Officials Say U.S. Bombing Kills 18
2003-04-06.abstract: An American warplane mistakenly attacked a convoy of American soldiers, senior Kurdish commanders and journalists today in northern Iraq.

2003-04-06.title: Showing Flag, Testing Foe
2003-04-06.abstract: Michael R Gordon analysis of US Army's audacious drive through streets of Baghdad; says it was intended to demonstrate to residents that American military is at gates of city and determined to operate inside, as well as to test mettle of Iraqi defenders and clear path for similar forays in future (M)

2003-04-07.title: 'Bush Must Present a Peace Plan'
2003-04-07.abstract: Former American national security advisor Zbigniew Brzezinski, 75, talks about a post-war order for Iraq, a possible end to the conflict in the Middle East, and overcoming the quarrels between America and Europe.

2003-04-07.title: Anti-Americanism in Greece Is Reinvigorated by War
2003-04-07.abstract: War in Iraq drives up anti-American feeling in Greece; one poll shows 94 percent of Greeks oppose war against Iraq, and another poll shows that more Greeks have positive view of Saddam Hussein than of Pres Bush and that majority of respondents believe US is as undemocratic at Iraq; photo (M)

2003-04-07.title: Freed P.O.W. Has Reunion With Family
2003-04-07.abstract: Defense Department announces deaths of four more Marines in Iraq: Pfc Chad E Bales, Cpl Mark A Evnin, Capt Benjamin W Sammis and Cpl Erik H Silva; their families comment; photos (M)

2003-04-07.title: A Face-Lift for Down-at-Heel Aeroflot
2003-04-07.abstract: Aeroflot of Russia is staking claim as global force to be recognized, after decade of malaise; its 110-plane fleet will be repainted and flight attendants' uniforms will be replaced with snazzy designer creations; other improvements noted; new Airbus and Boeing jets are on way to supplement Russian models that make up bulk of fleet; history of Aeroflot recalled; photos (M)

2003-04-07.title: TRANSITION PLANS
2003-04-07.abstract: Deputy Defense Sec Paul D Wolfowitz says it may take United States more than six months to cede power in postwar Iraq to Iraqi-led civilian authority that would pave way for representative government; recalls that after Persian Gulf war in 1991 it took six months for Kurdish forces in northern Iraq to establish effective self-governance; envisions no central postwar role for United Nations in political administration, as many European countries want; Gen Peter Pace, vice chairman of Joint Chiefs of Staff, tells reporters that American and British forces or 'those who might volunteer to join us' could provide 'secure environment' so that new Iraqi government could build its police force and its armed forces up to internal defense-type capability; Pres Bush prepares to head to Belfast, Northern Ireland, on Apr 7 for his third meeting in three weeks with Prime Min Tony Blair of Britain, who has pushed for deeper UN involvement in postwar Iraq; UN Sec Gen Kofi Annan calls Security Council meeting to discuss Iraq; photo (M)

2003-04-08.title: Grim Scene for G.I.'s Raiding Iraqi Base
2003-04-08.abstract: It took an anti-tank missile to blow a hole through the steel-reinforced wall surrounding the Special Republican Guard's headquarters at the foot of this hill overlooking the capital.

2003-04-08.title: Warm Welcome and Stubborn Resistance for Marines
2003-04-08.abstract: Thousands of US marines pour into Baghdad after capturing bridge over Diyala River in daylong firefight, battling tenacious opposition to move about one mile into southeastern corner of Iraqi capital; photos; map; entry of Marine units is heavy psychological and military blow to government, making increasinly absurd its insistence that no American troops are in city, whereas in fact, they have entered from two sides (M)

2003-04-08.title: Bush Sees Limited Role for U.N. in Postwar Iraq
2003-04-08.abstract: President Bush described the U.N.'s role as largely humanitarian and advisory rather than one central to overseeing postwar Iraq.

2003-04-08.title: Question of Hussein's Survival Still Uncertain
2003-04-08.abstract: Pentagon officials said that they could not answer what, for the moment, is the biggest question of all about the war in Iraq: is Saddam Hussein dead or alive?

2003-04-08.title: Bombs Attack on Hussein, a Bush-Blair Meeting, and Protests at Home
2003-04-08.abstract: As President Saddam Hussein's control over Iraq appeared to crumble further, American forces made an attempt to kill him with a precision bombing strike. Meanwhile, American troops seized two of Mr. Hussein's palaces in the capital and British troops consolidated their control of Iraq's second-largest city, Basra. Rapid military advances added urgency to a meeting between President Bush and Prime Minister Tony Blair over rebuilding Iraq.

2003-04-09.title: Israeli Forces Kill 5 Palestinians in Gaza
2003-04-09.abstract: The Israeli military in tanks and other armored vehicles charged into the northern Gaza Strip and shot dead five Palestinians today following Palestinian rocket fire on an Israeli town.

2003-04-09.title: Casualties
2003-04-09.abstract: Tally of American, British and Iraqi casualties in Iraq war

2003-04-09.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: 3 Convicted In Bombing Campaign
2003-04-09.abstract: Real IRA members Noel Maguire and brothers Robert and Aiden Hulme are found guilty in 2001 London bombings; car bombs resulted in extensive property damage, but no deaths (S)

2003-04-09.title: 'Today the Regime Is in Disarray,' U.S. Military Says
2003-04-09.abstract: Saying that the war is not over, a senior commander declared today that Saddam Hussein's government had lost control of Baghdad.

2003-04-09.title: White House Proclaims That Hussein's Rule Is Collapsing
2003-04-09.abstract: Several senior administration officials made public statements today applauding the war's progress and warning that danger lies ahead.

2003-04-10.title: A Beijing Doctor Questions Data on Illness
2003-04-10.abstract: Dr Jiang Yanyong, senior retired military physician, says China's health ministry is lying about number of people hospitalized in Beijing with severe acute respiratory syndrome; says number in military hospitals alone could be 'up to 100'; health ministry has reported only 12 cases and 3 deaths in Beijing; another doctor, speaking on condition of anonymity, claims there are dozens of patients at one nonmilitary hospital in Beijing; fact that so many cases have gone unreported in Beijing underscores China's continuing lack of openness in confronting outbreak that has left 1,279 people ill and killed 53 in China; worldwide, tally is 2,750 infected and 103 dead; photo (M)

2003-04-10.title: 3 U.S. Health Workers Said to Be Infected
2003-04-10.abstract: Officials of Centers for Disease Control and Prevention report three health care workers in US have caught mysterious respiratary disease SARS while caring for patients in this country; will not disclose where cases occurred; CDC has previously reported separate instances in which patients with disease have transmitted it to six household members in this country (M)

2003-04-10.title: Iraq's Border With Jordan Remains Chaotic
2003-04-10.abstract: Almost until the last minute the border post in Trbeel functioned with the efficiency and graft of Saddam Hussein's Iraq.

2003-04-10.title: Relief Agencies Are Forced to Wait as Chaos Reigns
2003-04-10.abstract: Red Cross official Vatche Arslanian is killed in crossfire in Baghdad, forcing it and other agencies to suspend relief program; full relief effort will be carried out by United States civilian agencies led by US Agency for International Development and will include agencies from UN and other international and private relief groups; by charter, these agencies are unable to work under authority of any military force, including Pentagon's civilian team headed by retired Gen Jay Garner, which will initially form administration; agencies will coordinate with Garner to ensure programs are as effective as possible; are stockpiling supplies but say they have little idea what they will find when they are able to inspect sanitation and water systems, size of food stocks and medical system; effort will require 480,000 tons of food every month and will cost nearly $2 billion; photo (M)

2003-04-10.title: Focus Shifts to Northern Iraq; A Message for Iraqis; U.S. Says Baghdad 'Still an Ugly Place'
2003-04-10.abstract: Several hundred Kurdish fighters under the command of 100 American special force fighters took control of the oil town of Kirkuk today as the focus of the American campaign switched to the northern part of Iraq.

2003-04-11.title: Northern Irish Peace Hopes Are Set Back
2003-04-11.abstract: Hopes of breakthrough in deadlocked Northern Ireland peace process are dashed when Prime Mins Tony Blair of Britain and Bertie Ahern of Ireland are forced to cancel joint appearance because of last-minute objections by Sinn Fein, political party of Irish Republican Army; new plan drawn up in London and Dublin is aimed at producing long-awaited decision by IRA to disarm and renounce violence for good (S)

2003-04-11.title: Fall of a Northern City, Suicide Bombing on Marines, Killing of a Cleric
2003-04-11.abstract: Summary of major developments in Iraq war (M)

2003-04-11.title: A Media Campaign Is Intended to Speed End of War
2003-04-11.abstract: Pres George Bush and Prime Min Tony Blair use newly seized Iraqi state television facilities to send message to Iraqi people promising to help build 'government that protects the rights of all citizens'; message is opening salvo in Pentagon-devised media campaign intended to shape messages sent to Iraqi people and speed end of war; it is unclear how many people received broadcast because of widespread power failures; both governments also intend to publish newspapers; details of other information dissemination campaigns noted, including broadcast of American nightly news programs; photos (M)

2003-04-11.title: Mosul Falls as Iraqis Surrender, Leaving Tikrit as Last Holdout
2003-04-11.abstract: American forces entered Mosul today after it fell without a fight as the last of Saddam Hussein's loyalists vanished during the night.

2003-04-11.title: U.S. Envoy Urges I.R.A. to Rescue Northern Ireland Peace Plan
2003-04-11.abstract: LONDON, April 11 — President Bush's special envoy to Northern Ireland, Richard N. Haass, said tonight that it was up to the Irish Republican Army to make a

2003-04-12.title: Sniper Fire Greets G.I.'s in Big City in North
2003-04-12.abstract: American Special Forces troops enter Mosul, Iraq, after organized Iraqi resistance flees, finding city engulfed by anarchy; quickly retreat; incursion followed talks with tribal and Baath Party leaders intended to bring about peaceful surrender of city, homeland of Sunni Arab nationalism; armed Kurds in civilian clothes drive around city flying flag of Kurdish Democratic Party; thousands of Kurdish troops remain outside city; Kurds in city stage small-scale uprising; city's Arabs begin attacking Americans; Arabs and Kurds blame US for not moving soldiers into Mosul quickly enough to stop looting; photo (M)

2003-04-12.title: Freed G.I. to Return To U.S. to Undergo Further Treatment
2003-04-12.abstract: Officials at military hospital in Germany say Pfc Jessica D Lynch has recovered enough from her injuries to return to United States, where she will undergo further medical treatment; she was rescued from Iraqi captivity on Apr 1 (S)

2003-04-12.title: Marines in Nowhere Land: Sentry Duty in the Desert
2003-04-12.abstract: Two Marine sentries charged with protecting Camp Fenway in Iraq sit in foxhole in desert on cold night; their physical discomfort described; away from camaraderie of camp, wind becomes their friend, but silence is not to be trusted (M)

2003-04-12.title: World Briefing | Asia: Cambodia: Relations With Thailand Resume
2003-04-12.abstract: Cambodia and Thailand agree to reinstate diplomatic relations, which have been suspended since Thai embassy and businesses were destroyed and vandalized in last January Phnom Penh rioting; riots were brought on by rumors that well-known Thai actress Suwanan Khongying had claimed Angkor temples belong to Thailand; Cambodian government has paid Thailand $5.9 million for damages done to embassy (S)

2003-04-12.title: Battling the spread of SARS, Asian nations escalate travel restrictions
2003-04-12.abstract: HONG KONG:—  Asian nations battling the spread of a killer disease across land, sea and air borders imposed dramatic new restrictions Friday on all travelers suspected of infection.

2003-04-13.title: Casualties
2003-04-13.abstract: Table shows casualties in Iraq war: 110 US troops, 31 British, 16 US troops captured or missing

2003-04-13.title: Syrian Foreign Minister Denies Covert Aid to Iraq
2003-04-13.abstract: Syrian Foreign Min Farouk al-Sharaa denies series of accusations leveled by United State about covert Syrian assistance to Iraq and criticizes American military for failing to establish order there; photo of Sharaa with French Foreign Min Dominique de Villepin (M)

2003-04-13.title: Bush Accuses Syria of Harboring Senior Iraqis
2003-04-13.abstract: President Bush also accused Syria of possessing chemical weapons, but he was careful not to threaten military force.

2003-04-13.title: Turks and Kurds Maintain a Mutual Suspicion
2003-04-13.abstract: Almost as soon as Kurdish forces streamed into the northern Iraqi city of Kirkuk this week, American diplomats were on the phone to Turkish government officials to assure them that allied forces would take control of the situation.    That pledge had an immediate practical goal: to keep Turkish troops from entering the region, thus preventing the possibility of clashes between Turkish soldiers and Iraqi Kurds.

2003-04-13.title: For Allies, the Next Target Is Hussein's Hometown
2003-04-13.abstract: All roads now lead to Tikrit for United States forces in Iraq; Tikrit is once-bleak farming town that Saddam Hussein imprinted with his opulent style of mosques and palaces; it may be last place that his loyalists could mount fierce battle against allied forces, a concern among American officials (M)

2003-04-14.title: Bush Demands 'Cooperation' From Syrians
2003-04-14.abstract: Pres Bush accuses Syria of harboring senior Iraqi military and government officials; demands 'cooperation' in punishing some of them, but stops short of threatening to use military force against Syria; asserts there are chemical weapons in Syria but does not clarify whether they were shipped there during or before war in Iraq; Syrian officials deny allegations that they are taking in Iraqi leaders and accuse Washington of waging 'disinformation campaign' to divert attention from lawlessness in Iraq; Bush also says efforts to get North Korea to bargaining table to discuss nuclear arms may be successful; question remains whether US march to Baghdad will drive North Korea to disarm or to build nuclear arsenal; Gen Tommy Franks says military has samples of Saddam Hussein's DNA and will use them to determine whether he has been killed in airstrikes (M)

2003-04-14.title: Mideast Sides Maneuver, Expecting New Peace Effort
2003-04-14.abstract: Adversaries in Israeli-Palestinian conflict work to strengthen their diplomatic footing, anticipating renewed push by Bush administration for Middle East peace as war in Iraq winds down; Prime Min Ariel Sharon of Israel seeks substantial revisions in peace plan that Pres Bush pledges to publish soon; says plan should include requirement that Palestinians give up their claim of right of return to what is now Israel for refugees of 1948 war that created state; new Palestinian Prime Min Mahmaud Abbas struggles to form government, Bush's condition for moving ahead with plan; his proposed list of ministers would take crucial posts out of hands of men tied to Yasir Arafat; Arafat and his Fatah movement object, and are delaying scheduled presentation of list to Al Fatah's central committee; Abbas's list also comes under criticism from Palestinian reformers, who say too many old faces remain; photo (M)

2003-04-14.title: Liberal Party Leads Separatists in Quebec Election Poll
2003-04-14.abstract: Canada's federalist Liberal Party jumps to lead in polls and appears poised to wrest Quebec provincial government from separatist Parti Quebecois in Apr 14 elections; victory could portend big change in Canadian politics; shift appears to reflect emerging consensus on reducing local taxes, tackling long lines for publicly financed health care and, most importantly, ending Parti Quebecois dream of separating from Canada; polls that show Liberals surging rapidly also suggest that Parti Quebecois could possibly make last-minute comeback and retain control of provincial assembly it has held since 1994; photo (M)

2003-04-14.title: The Internet Is Awash in Ads for Products Promising Cures or Protection
2003-04-14.abstract: Federal health officials warn consumers to be wary of advertisements on Internet for products claiming to prevent SARS; Food and Drug Administration Comr Mark B McClellen says risk of contracting disease in United States is 'very, very low,' and there is no evidence that any specific drug is effective against it; photos (M)

2003-04-14.title: U.S. TROOPS POISED TO OUST LOYALISTS IN NORTHERN CITY
2003-04-14.abstract: American marines move into edges of Tikrit, ancestral home of Saddam Hussein, and meet significant resistance; report getting 'foothold' in city; bombardment by jets and helicopter gunships is intense; objective is to wipe out what is believed to be one of last large concentrations of forces loyal to government of Hussein, and do it in two or three days; size of Iraqi force inside Tikrit is estimated to be about 2,500 men; Tikrit is thought to be where several high-ranking military leaders and possibly members of Hussein's inner circle have taken refuge; city is near village of Awja, Hussein's birthplace; southeast of Baghdad, marines enter town of Kut unopposed; British forces relieve First Marine Division in Amara, and now control area between Amara and Basra; no casualty figures are available for either side (M)

2003-04-15.title: Science Aids Hussein Hunt, General Says
2003-04-15.abstract: Any member of President Saddam Hussein's family can be positively identified, whether dead or alive, through scientific matching techniques.

2003-04-15.title: One-Day Death Rate From New Virus Hits 9 in Hong Kong
2003-04-15.abstract: The toll in Hong Kong from severe acute respiratory syndrome set a new single-day record.

2003-04-15.title: Casualties
2003-04-15.abstract: Tally of reported casualties, missing and captured in Iraq war as of April 14

2003-04-15.title: Spokeswoman in '91 War To Help U.S. Leader in Iraq
2003-04-15.abstract: Margaret Tutwiler leaves post as ambasador to Morocco to assume temporary position overseeing all public relations and information operations in postwar Iraq; was State Department spokeswoman during Persian Gulf war in 1991; photo (S)

2003-04-15.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-04-15.abstract: INDONESIA: Mediators Seek Talks On Restive Province; JAPAN: Masked In The Halls Of Power;.

2003-04-16.title: Italy Seeks Extradition of Captured Cruise Ship Hijacker
2003-04-16.abstract: Italian courts already handed down a conviction of multiple life terms to Abu Abbas for masterminding the 1985 hijacking of the Achille Lauro cruise liner.

2003-04-16.title: European Union's Expansion Is Overshadowed by Disarray
2003-04-16.abstract: Gathering of European Union leaders in Athens for signing of treaties that will enlarge group by 10 member was meant to showcase European unity, but that message is being overshadowed by rifts among members, particularly over war in Iraq, which Britain and Spain supported and France, Greece and Germany opposed; photo (M)

2003-04-16.title: South Africa to Pay $3,900 to Each Family of Apartheid Victims
2003-04-16.abstract: Pres Thabo Mbeki of South Africa says his government will pay reparations totaling $85 million to more than 19,000 victims of apartheid crimes who testified about their suffering before Truth and Reconciliation Commission; family of each victim will receive about $3,900, which compares with average annual salary in South Africa of about $3,000 (M)

2003-04-16.title: Experts' Pleas to Pentagon Didn't Save Museum
2003-04-16.abstract: Plunder of National Museum of Iraq, one of Middle East's most important archaeological repositories, occurred despite repeated pleas to Defense Department by experts and scholars that it be protected when American troops entered Baghdad; senior Pentagon officials say military agreed not to target site, but never guaranteed it would be able to guard it from looters (M)

2003-04-16.title: South Korea Abstains From Vote Assailing North on Rights
2003-04-16.abstract: South Korea abstained from voting on a resolution adopted by a U.N. commission that condemns North Korea for "widespread and grave" abuses of human rights.

2003-04-17.title: Marines Again Kill Iraqis In Exchange of Fire in Mosul
2003-04-17.abstract: Marines in Mosul, believing they are being fired upon, open fire in city's main square, killing four Iraqis and wounding 10; is second such incident in as many days; residents say shootings must stop and warn Americans not to squander whatever good will remains; photos (M)

2003-04-17.title: An Art Center Left in Ashes
2003-04-17.abstract: Baghdad's cultural elite rage about destruction of city's art treasures, first by American bombs and then by looters; Amal al-Khedairy, whose home is in one of Baghdad's wealthier enclaves, walks amid rubble and shattered windows and ransacked remains of her home and cultural center she ran; war has dragged her from comfortable way of life under Saddam Hussein; she does not speak of compromises involved in running Baghdad's most luminous artistic center that flourished during Hussein's reign; her rage seems emblematic of whole class of people who might be expected to be more sympathetic to American cause; all they can see now is ruins; photo (M)

2003-04-17.title: Strikes in the East
2003-04-17.abstract: Map of Iraq shows locations of military skirmishes

2003-04-17.title: U.S. Generals Meet in Palace, Sealing Victory
2003-04-17.abstract: Gen Tommy R Franks seals his victory in war on Iraq by convening top allied officers in one of Saddam Hussein's proudest palaces; commanders gathering at Abu Ghraib palace in Baghdad discuss how to maintain security and rebuild Iraq now that Hussein is gone; decide to meet again in Franks's headquarters in Qatar in several days to complete plans; agree that Army will take responsibility for Baghdad and territory to north, while Marines deploy in south, where British control Basra, most important city; two-hour session concludes with video conference with Pres Bush; meeting is laden with symbolism, making clear that Americans now dominate Iraq; American troops strive to re-establish order, but Baghdad remains shattered city still groping for normal life, still in thrall of looters and arsonists and full of wary and confused people with hostility toward Americans; Franks acknowledges that much remains to be done; photos; map (M)

2003-04-17.title: U.S. May Punish Cuba for Imprisoning Critics
2003-04-17.abstract: Bush administration is considering series of steps to punish Cuban government for its recent crackdown on dissidents; Pres Bush is likely to make public statement soon about crackdown, which could involve cutting off cash payments to relatives in Cuba or halting direct flights to island; he is expected to issue stern warning to Havana government that US will not tolerate another exodus of rafters; Fidel Castro has relieved internal tensions several times during his 44-year tenure by allowing mass migrations to Florida; officials and Cuba specialists speculate about why Castro has unleashed most sweeping crackdown on dissidents in years; some say Castro was unnerved by American-led ouster of his ally Saddam Hussein in Iraq; others note that dissident community has made some strides in recent months (M)

2003-04-18.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: A First
2003-04-18.abstract: Aneesa Mirza elected mayor of Ahmadabad, commercial capital of Gujarat State; becomes first Muslim woman mayor in India (S)

2003-04-18.title: One Airline's 5 Weeks With a Medical Threat
2003-04-18.abstract: Northwest Airlines' effort since March 15 to keep on top of rapidly emerging respiratory disease SARS reviewed; airline has crisis team that gets updates on spread of SARS and has ordered preventive measures such as wiping down ticket counters and allowing employees to wear masks and gloves; Northwest is particularly vulnerable as one of two major US airlines serving Asia; United declines to discuss procedures (M)

2003-04-18.title: Trained for War, 12 Green Berets Keep the Peace in an Iraqi Town
2003-04-18.abstract: US Army Special Forces soldiers in Diwaniya, who came to Iraq trained to fight war, find themselves wading into angry crowds, mediating between rival tribes and trying to hold together thin threads of social order; photo; 12-man team has become de facto center of government, hearing daily from tribal leaders, businesses and ordinary citizens who stream into compound to complain about spotty electricity, rampant looting and lack of jobs and commerce; Green Berets, several of whom speak Arabic, have in last week started police force, recruited English-speaking professor as city manager, located offices for municipal government and begun holding meetings where community leaders discuss problems (M)

2003-04-18.title: Moscow Gunman Kills Leader Of New Western-Style Party
2003-04-18.abstract: Unidentified gunman kills Sergei N Yushenkov, legislator and co-chairman of Western-style Liberal Russia Party, in front of Yushenkov's Moscow home; photo; second assassination of Liberal Russia leader in eight months occurs just after Yushenkov announces that new party will run candidates in parliamentary elections this December (M)

2003-04-18.title: In the Skies Over Iraq, Silent Observers Become Futuristic Weapons
2003-04-18.abstract: Remotely piloted aircraft like Predator and Global Hawk have played crucial role in Iraq war, providing bird's-eye view of battlefield and giving military planners ability to take out targets, all without risking American lives; about 15 are in use in skies above Iraq; diagram shows how aircraft gathers and transmits data (M)

2003-04-19.title: Facing U.S. Threat of Penalties, Cuba Issues a Defiant Statement
2003-04-19.abstract: Cuba issues defiant statement in face of Bush administration threats to penalize Cuba for jailing nonviolent dissidents; experts say economy is especially vulnerable and would suffer if United States stops family remittances, which amount to up to $1 billion a year (M)

2003-04-19.title: Young and Hungry Captives
2003-04-19.abstract: Their eyes widened when one of their jailers entered the room in Mahkmoor, Iraq, with a stack of fresh bread. Then, shamelessly, they devoured it, wolfing down chunk after chunk.

2003-04-19.title: Teacher, a Survivor, Fondly Recalls Life in Hussein's Iraq
2003-04-19.abstract: Montgomery Malaami, Assyrian Christian teacher in Basra, offers positive recollections of life in Saddam Hussein's Iraq, as long as one kept on good side of Baathists; photo (M)

2003-04-19.title: The Military in Transition: From Combat to Containment
2003-04-19.abstract: Photos and diagrams explaining how US military in Iraq is shifting tactics from large-scale hostilities to establishing street-level security and containing civil disorder

2003-04-19.title: Iraq's Neighbors Issue Declaration Criticizing U.S. Over Syria
2003-04-19.abstract: Foreign ministers of six countries that share border with Iraq, including Syria, hold first postwar meeting in Saudi Arabia and sharply criticize US for mounting pressure on Syria; ministers urge 'occupying powers' to quickly stabilize Iraq and withdraw; photo (M)

2003-04-20.title: Scenes That Linger: An Iraq Chronicle by the Witnesses to a War
2003-04-20.abstract: Overview of news coverage of war in Iraq by New York Times correspondents and photographers; photos (L)

2003-04-20.title: Questions Arise on Drafting Blueprint for Governing Iraq
2003-04-20.abstract: Question of who should write new Iraqi constitution and how it should be adopted is being discussed in aftermath of war; conference intended as first step toward creation of interim authority is held in Ur and is attended by Kanan Makiya, exile who has relationship with Bush administration, and other prominent expatriots; photo (M)

2003-04-20.title: Young Fighters on a Mature Mission
2003-04-20.abstract: Excerpts of diary of photographer Ozier Muhammad, who witnessed war in Iraq firsthand; recalls experience with Marine Division when explosions shook area where they were sleeping; photos (M)

2003-04-20.title: A New Way of Warfare Leaves Behind an Abundance of Loose Ends
2003-04-20.abstract: News analysis: war in Iraq was neither as painful as opponents predicted nor as painless as proponents suggested; consequences for world and regional political stability are still unknowable; new flexibility in fighting and intelligence gave allied forces edge, but there were also some nasty surprises and speed of operation contributed to plethora of loose ends, not least of which are questions about fate of Saddam Hussein and existence of weapons of mass destruction; how exile Ahmad Chalabi will be received in effort to form government and impact of destruction of historical relics and libraries also remain to be seen; in all, American triumph is not as clear-cut as was hoped; photo (M)

2003-04-20.title: The War Through the Eyes of Times Reporters
2003-04-20.abstract: DAVID ROHDEYoung and Hungry Captives Their eyes widened when one of their jailers entered the room in Mahkmoor, Iraq, with a stack of fresh bread. Then, shamelessly, they devoured it, wolfing down chunk after chunk.

2003-04-21.title: Bush More Hopeful on Syria
2003-04-21.abstract: President Bush said that he was encouraged by signs that Syria was heeding his call not to harbor former Iraqi leaders.

2003-04-21.title: Leader's Collapse Raises Concern for Azerbaijan's Health
2003-04-21.abstract: The 79-year-old president of Azerbaijan collapsed twice during a nationally televised speech there today, prompting fresh questions about the health of a leader considered integral to the stability of his oil-rich country.

2003-04-21.title: Bush Now Says He Believes Syria Wants to Cooperate
2003-04-21.abstract: Pres Bush says he believes Syria is willing to cooperate with US, primarily about providing harbor to senior members of Saddam Hussein's administration; also appears to address broader concerns of Syria's links to terrorist organizations and development of chemical weapons; comments come amid debate over how long US will have to occupy Iraq and how it can retain control over Iraq's political and economic development; photo (M)

2003-04-21.title: 'Let Them Arrest Him,' Tariq Aziz's Aunt Says
2003-04-21.abstract: Selma Dawood, aunt of Tariq Aziz, disavows him and says relationship was cursory at best; Aziz, who has been missing since invasion of Baghdad, was prime minister under Saddam Hussein and well known worldwide as Iraq's voice; Dawood praises American action in Iraq; map; photo (M)

2003-04-21.title: Serbia Cracks Down on Mobsters and War-Crime Suspects
2003-04-21.abstract: Serbian government is cracking down on gangsters and criminals under state of emergency imposed after assassination of reformist Prime Min Zoran Djindjic on March 12; police have questioned more than 7,000 people and imprisoned more than 2,000 suspected members of what Serbs like to call the mafia; dragnet is also sweeping up some of most notorious figures from Slobodan Milosevic era, including top army and security officials and others accused of war crimes (M)

2003-04-22.title: Opposition Figures Team Up To Take On Taiwan President
2003-04-22.abstract: The leaders of Taiwan's two main opposition parties, which together have outpolled the current party in power in recent elections, have agreed to run together on a single ticket in next year's presidential election.    The agreement, between Lien Chan of the Nationalist Party and James Soong of the People First Party, comes after years of personal rivalry. The decision by Mr. Soong to seek the vice presidency as Mr. Lien's running mate, instead of running again for the presidency, gives the two men a strong chance of beating President Chen Shui-bian when he seeks re-election in March, political analysts here said.

2003-04-22.title: Reported SARS Cases in Beijing Take Sharp 2-Day Jump
2003-04-22.abstract: Reported cases in Beijing of SARS climbed by 249 to 588 as the government continued its belated campaign to give honest figures.

2003-04-22.title: World Briefing | United Nations: Argentine Is Elected To New Court
2003-04-22.abstract: Argentine attorney Luis Moreno Ocampo is elected chief prosecutor of newly established permanent Hague war crimes court, which is expected to begin hearing cases next year; photo (S)

2003-04-22.title: Azerbaijan's Leader Collapses While Delivering TV Speech
2003-04-22.abstract: The 79-year-old president of Azerbaijan, Heydar A. Aliyev, collapsed twice during a nationally televised speech there today, prompting fresh questions about the health of a leader considered integral to the stability of his oil-producing country.    Mr. Aliyev, who has a history of heart and other health problems, was addressing a military audience in Baku, the capital of Azerbaijan, when he gasped, clutched his heart, complained of pain and fell. The live television broadcast of his remarks was interrupted while bodyguards rushed over to support him.

2003-04-22.title: Elated Shiites, On Pilgrimage, Want U.S. Out
2003-04-22.abstract: In an extraordinary display of the raw power of Iraq's Shiites, the country's long repressed yet dominant Islamic sect, hundreds of thousands converged on this holy city today to perform one of the most important rituals in the Shiite calendar for the first time in a quarter of a century.    The mass pilgrimage, banned under Saddam Hussein, carries as much political as religious significance for the Shiites, who, though they make up the majority of Iraqis, have long lived as second-class citizens under the rule of a Sunni Muslim minority. The procession has a history of turning into a political rally and many who gathered here for the festival that culminates on Wednesday were eager to express their desire to choose their own government without the interference of the United States.

2003-04-23.title: Pentagon Reports Raising $1.7 Billion to Assist Relief Effort
2003-04-23.abstract: Pentagon raises $1.7 billion in financial assistance, food, medicine and other relief products for Iraq from various countries, including Jordan, United Arab Emirates, Britain, Japan, Australia, Netherlands, Canada, Switzerland and Romania (M)

2003-04-23.title: Gingrich Denounces State Department
2003-04-23.abstract: Newt Gingrich delivers scathing attack on Bush administration in speech to American Enterprises Institute, charging that State Department under Sec of State Colin L Powell is guilty of pattern of diplomatic failure that weakened America's standing in world and threatens to weaken effects of military victory in Iraq; scores department for enlisting Russia, European Union and United Nations to write plan to establish Palestinian state (S)

2003-04-23.title: SARS Cases In Hong Kong Have Begun To Level Off
2003-04-23.abstract: Health officials report number of people infected with severe acute respiratory syndrom has leveled off in Hong Kong in last week, although disease is unlikely to be eradicated soon, if ever; Hong Kong still has two to three dozen new cases a day and almost as many people hospitalized with SARS as rest of world; area's number of hospitalized probable SARS cases peaked at 960 and has since fallen 9 percent; 34 SARS patients have died, most of them elderly; tentative signs are emerging that residents are accepting SARS as manageable risk and moving on with daily lives; graph of hospitalized patients; photo (M)

2003-04-23.title: Hong Kong Earmarks $1.5 Billion to Deal With Effects of SARS
2003-04-23.abstract: The plan calls for government spending and tax cuts concentrated in the next four months that will equal 1 percent of the territory's entire annual economic output.

2003-04-23.title: World Briefing | Americas: Cuba: Trade Pact Threatened
2003-04-23.abstract: European Union spokesman holds that Cuba's recent crackdown on opposition, evidenced by execution of 3 men for hijacking and imprisonment of 75 dissidents, jeopardizes potential trade relations with European Union; holds that damaged relationship is not irreparable as union foreign ministers will not vote on deal with Cuba until June (S)

2003-04-24.title: Virus Proves Baffling, Turning Up in Only 40% of a Lab's Test Cases
2003-04-24.abstract: Canada's National Microbiology Laboratory finds virus for severe acute respiratory syndrome in only 40 percent of probable and suspected cases, suprisingly low rate; additionally, portion of recent cases testing positive for virus is declining, and a number of people who are not suspected of having SARS are testing positive; director Dr Frank Plummer says findings have potential to weaken link in Canada between SARS and the previously unknown member of coronavirus family that World Health Organization has said is cause of SARS; stresses he is not challenging WHO's conclusion, but finds results 'weird'; WHO says it continues to believe that new coronavirus is cause of SARS, but is following research developments (M)

2003-04-24.title: China Offers Its Help in U.S.-North Korea Nuclear Talks
2003-04-24.abstract: American envoys complete first day of talks to persuade North Korea to abandon its nuclear weapons program, and China appears to be playing unusually assertive role in keeping lines of communication open; officials and analysts describe China as determined to keep talks going, to soften entrenched positions and to force both sides to focus on how to bring about diplomatic outcome; photo (M)

2003-04-24.title: Afghan President Shows Cards In Pakistan's Taliban Deck
2003-04-24.abstract: Afghani Pres Hamid Karzai asks Pakistan to hand over Taliban members that Afghanistan accuses of being terrorists and says Pakistan is sheltering; names specific Taliban commanders who he says are guilty of atrocities and also behind continuing campaign of violent attacks against Afghan government; Karzai is on two-day state visit to Pakistan; map (M)

2003-04-24.title: Clue on Missing Gulf War Pilot Is Said to Be Found
2003-04-24.abstract: WASHINGTON (CNN) -- After an initial search of several sites in Iraq, U.S. investigators have found a clue indicating a U.S. Navy pilot who was shot down in the Gulf War may have been alive years after the fact, Pentagon officials told CNN Wednesday.

2003-04-24.title: Tariq Aziz Surrenders; Interim Government Near
2003-04-24.abstract: Tariq Aziz, the Iraqi deputy prime minister who often served as the public face of Saddam Hussein's government, surrendered to American forces.

2003-04-25.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-04-25.abstract: THE PHILIPPINES: Counterattack Against Rebels; INDONESIA: Peace Talks Collapse; JAPAN: Death Sought For Cult Leader; CAMBODIA: Neighbors Reconcile;.

2003-04-25.title: Japan's Shrine to War Dead Worries About Its Generation Gap
2003-04-25.abstract: Annual Great Spring Festival of Yasukuni Shrine is held to dwindling group of aging relatives of Japan's war dead; it is most hotly disputed shrine of Japan's oldest religion, because mixed in with heroes, celebrated and unknown, are 14 Class A war criminals associated with atrocities committed during World War II; visits to shrine by prime ministers and lesser politicians regularly cause diplomatic crises with Japan's most important neighbors, China and Koreas (M)

2003-04-25.title: World Briefing: Africa
2003-04-25.abstract: ETHIOPIA: Food Shortages Worsen; SUDAN: Tribal Violence;.

2003-04-25.title: To Broad Support, Hong Kong Police Take On an Expanded Role in Fighting SARS
2003-04-25.abstract: Government of Hong Kong is assigning broad and expanding role to police in effort to fight spread of infectious disease SARS; officers are taking computer software designed for major criminal investigations and instead entering information on family members and other close contacts of people with SARS; other officers pay three visits daily to homes of people ordered not to go out because someone in their household has confirmed case of disease; new confinement orders are issued for household members of suspected SARS patients as well; tougher measures appear to have broad support in populace terrified of disease that is still infecting 200 more people and killing two dozen each week; photo (M)

2003-04-25.title: Suicide Bomber Kills a Guard and Wounds 13 Israelis
2003-04-25.abstract: Palestinian suicide bomber blows himself up when confronted by Israeli security guard at train station in Kfar Saba, Israel, killing guard and wounding 13 Israelis; bombing drives home challenge facing new Palestinian prime minister, Mahmoud Abbas, who will be expected to rein in militants as part of peace initiative; his new government is likely to be formally approved within week, move that will likely bring on peace initiative sponsored by United States, United Nations, European Union and Russia; Israel says any diplomatic progress is contingent on Palestinians' subduing militants; still, Israeli response to attack in Kfar Saba is relatively restrained; Prime Min Ariel Sharon, who shunned Yasir Arafat, is prepared to meet Abbas, opening channel for dialogue that has been largely absent for last two years; militant groups say they will continue their attacks; they claim new government is being forced on Palestinians by US and other foreign govenrments; photo; map (M)

2003-04-26.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-04-26.abstract: PAKISTAN: 13 Are Killed In Crash; THAILAND: A Dream Flight ;.

2003-04-26.title: World Briefing | Asia: Thailand: A Dream Flight
2003-04-26.abstract: Thai Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra is passenger in F-16 fighter jet enroute to air show (S)

2003-04-26.title: Winnie Mandela Is Sentenced to Four Years for Theft and Fraud
2003-04-26.abstract: Winnie Madikizela-Mandela, former wife of Nelson Mandela, is sentenced to four years in prison on dozens of counts of theft and fraud; Addy Moolman, broker and business associate, is sentenced to seven years in prison; Judge Peet Johnson acknowledges Madikizela-Mandela's contributions to building of South Africa's first democracy (M)

2003-04-26.title: Iraqi Civilians Killed in Blast at Ammunition Storage Site
2003-04-26.abstract: At least six Iraqis were killed today after an attack on an ammunition dump on the outskirts of the city set off powerful explosions in a residential area, the U.S. military said.

2003-04-26.title: Out of the Slums of Rio, an Author Finds Fame
2003-04-26.abstract: Profile of Paulo Lins, whose book City of God, and movie of same name, portray extreme violence and poverty of Rio's slums, where Lins grew up in public housing project in 1960's; interview; photo (M)

2003-04-27.title: A Muddled Electoral Field for Weary Argentines to Cross
2003-04-27.abstract: Argentines are set to vote to elect new president, their 6th in little more than year; polls indicate race, among nearly 20 candidates, is too close to call; ex-Pres Carlos Saul Menem narrowly leads field, with Ricardo Lopez Murphy and Nestor Kirchner in dead heat for right to take part in runoff; photos (M)

2003-04-27.title: Focusing One Hospital's Resources on One Disease
2003-04-27.abstract: Singapore has designated 1,000-bed hospital for SARS, disease that spreads so readily in closed spaces that patients are evaluated outside, in tent; hospital got its first case Mar 1, before disease was known, and many doctors fell ill; hospital now uses four specially ventilated rooms for each patient to insure isolation; medical staff wears special protective garb; photos (M)

2003-04-27.title: Greeks and Turks Mingle Peacefully on Cyprus
2003-04-27.abstract: Flood of Greek and Turk Cypriots are crossing to see each other's side of island divided for almost 30 years, rushing as if uncertain sudden opening will last; respond to abrupt lifting of travel restrictions and opening of two checkpoints by Turkish Cypriot leaders, move that surprised United Nations peacekeepers, police, diplomats and others; menacing buffer zone is still in place, but island is already seeming less divided in people's minds; map; photos (M)

2003-04-27.title: Dressed Up Amid Disorder, Unarmed Officers Stand Idle
2003-04-27.abstract: Baghdad's police force is being slowly reorganized after disbanding in face of US advance, but neighborhood police stations and their officers, who once provided reliable local presence, are still mostly unarmed and waiting for orders; photo (S)

2003-04-27.title: The New Face of France's Far Right
2003-04-27.abstract: Marine Le Pen is softer, younger side of France's National Front, led by her father, Jean-Marie Le Pen, who has named her vice president; photo; 34-year-old lawyer is most visible spokesperson for far right-wing party, pushing hard in interviews to 'de-demonize' its hard-line image; interview (M)

2003-04-28.title: Veteran TV Anchors Push to Become the Voices of a New Iraq
2003-04-28.abstract: Two of Iraq's prominent television news anchors turn up at their war-ruined studios in Baghdad, dressed up and ready for cameras to roll again; they worked for Shabab Television, pet project of Saddam Hussein's son Uday; two portray themselves merely as employees; photo (M)

2003-04-28.title: Rumsfeld Visits Troops in Qatar
2003-04-28.abstract: The defense secretary told troops at Central Command that they had proved doubters wrong by carrying out a war plan that quickly toppled Saddam Hussein's regime.

2003-04-28.title: Outbreak of Disease Brings Big Drop-Off In China's Economy
2003-04-28.abstract: Outbreak of new respiratory disease SARS inflicts greatest blow to Chinese economy since Tiananmen Square killings in 1989, causing plunge in retail sales, slump in demand for some Chinese exports and near-collapse in domestic and foreign tourism; Chinese economy has gone from expanding at annual rate of 9.9 percent in first quarter to shrinking at annual rate of 2 percent in second quarter, according to estimates from J P Morgan Chase; economic damage is being increased by some government policies adopted to slow spread of SARS, such as ordering closing of all movie theaters, Internet cafes and other places of entertainment in Beijing, or refusing entry to tour groups in Guangdong Province; if current economic downturn proves prolonged, it could damage Communist Party's claim to be best way to offer greater prosperity to 1.3 billion Chinese; photos; graph (L)

2003-04-28.title: Paraguay's Voters Appear to Extend Party's 5-Decade Rule
2003-04-28.abstract: Paraguayan voters appear to be putting their country back in hands of Colorado Party, whose rule has been unbroken since 1947; opposition parties had viewed this election as their best chance of unseating Colorados, with government corruption rife and economy sliding deeper into crisis; Colorado presidential candidate Nicanor Duarte Frutos, apparent winner, campaigned cleverly, playing on people's fears of rising crime and on nostalgia for old days; also called on Colorados' political machine, which prevades nearly all sectors of society, controlling state sector jobs and supplying rural communities with many of their needs; Duarte Frutos, in his victory speech, promises to crack down on corruption (M)

2003-04-28.title: As Hussein Faded, Prisoners Were Executed
2003-04-28.abstract: Saddam Hussein's dying government continued to execute suspected spies even as American troops neared huge prison at Abu Ghraib; Iraqis have almost never learned fate of loved ones executed in Hussein's 24 years as president; with Hussein gone, relatives descend on pit just outside prison, unearthing 14 bodies of recently killed prisoners; neighbors say they found 10 more corpses on prison grounds, also recently killed; personal stories told in two weeks since Hussein's fall reveal scale of torture and killing that may surpass predictions of even his worst critics; former inmates say last prisoners at Abu Ghraib were held as spies; thousands of other prisoners there were released in Oct in amnesty declared by Hussein as tensions between Iraq and US grew; photos of Iraqis, with faces covered against stench, digging up shallow graves of executed prisoners (M)

2003-04-29.title: World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: Elections In Doubt
2003-04-29.abstract: Politicians begin campaigns for May 29 elections for Northern Ireland Assembly despite continuing suspension of legislative body and talks to suspend elections as well; assembly was suspended last October after allegations of IRA paramilitary activity (S)

2003-04-29.title: Health Group Relies on a Time-Tested Rule to Determine Last New Case
2003-04-29.abstract: World Health Orgn is relying on time-tested rule of thumb used by epidemiologists in declaring Vietnam first country to stop its outbreak of SARS; rule says epidemic is over if no new cases occur in time span that is double longest known incubation period of the disease; SARS has 10-day incubation period and no new cases have been detected in Vietnam in 20 days; Vietnam had total of 63 cases and 5 deaths; WHO spokesman gives credit for Vietnam's success to old-fashioned infection-control measures; says there was high level of political commitment there from beginning (M)

2003-04-29.title: Full Text: In Bush's Words
2003-04-29.abstract: Following is a full transcript from a speech about Iraq by President Bush yesterday in Dearborn, Mich., as transcribed by Federal News Service Inc.

2003-04-29.title: Iraqis Set to Meet to Pick Transitional Government
2003-04-29.abstract: Political gathering of 300 Iraqis from most parts of country and most religious and ethnic factions, sponsored by United States and Britain, decides to call national conference in month's time to select postwar transitional government for Iraq; national conference is to decide whether Iraq wil adopt leadership council or single head of state; Zalmay Khalilzad, Bush administration envoy, is co-chairman of meeting, held in Baghdad; members of Shiite clerical groups are present, as are members of Supreme Council for the Islamic Revolution in Iraq, group with strong ties to Iran that boycotted earlier meeting in Nasiriya; several miles from conference hall, several thousand protesters affiliated with Shiite theological college in Najaf denounce meeting as unrepresentative of religious Shiism; security for Baghdad gathering is extraordinarily tight, partly because large-scale demonstrations are expected to mark Saddam Hussein's 66th birthday; in Tikrit and nearby Awja, Hussein's hometown, demonstrations that members of his family called for are thwarted by stepped-up presence of American troops; two nights earlier, pro-Hussein demonstration in Tikrit left one Iraq man injured and three Iraqi cars crushed by American tanks (M)

2003-04-29.title: Moussaoui Should Get Details In '5th Plane' Theory, Judge Says
2003-04-29.abstract: Judge Leonie M Brinkema orders Justice Dept to provide Zacarias Moussaoui with details to support its theory that he was meant to participate in terrorist hijacking separate from four hijackings that occurred on Sept 11, 2001, in plane that was to crash into White House; he is only person charged in American court with conspiring in Sept 11 attacks; was not allowed to attend hearing in which prosecutors outlined their theory because it involved classified information; Moussaoui acknowledges he was member of Qaeda network and loyal to Osama bin Laden, but he denies any involvement in Sept 11 attacks (M)

2003-04-30.title: Stocks Rise in Asia on Hopes That Worst of SARS Is Over
2003-04-30.abstract: Stock markets rise sharply across East Asia amid hopes that SARS outbreak can be controlled; photo of Hong Kong traders wearing face masks, which are becoming less common on floor of exchange and in streets in city hardest hit by disease; 12 more deaths reported there, bringing total to 150, but there are just 15 new cases (M)

2003-04-30.title: Just Too Late: Huge U.S. Bomb
2003-04-30.abstract: Pentagon sent two of its new 21,500-pound bombs to Persian Gulf but they arrived too late to be used in Iraq war (S)

2003-04-30.title: U.S. TO WITHDRAW ALL COMBAT UNITS FROM SAUDI ARABIA
2003-04-30.abstract: US will withdraw all combat forces from Saudi Arabia by this summer, ending military presence that began as joint operation to contain Saddam Hussein after 1991 Persian Gulf war but has become dangerous for US troops because of terrorism stoked by Osama bin Laden; US anger has also swelled since Sept 11 terrorist attacks in which 15 of 19 hijackers were Saudi; Sec Donald Rumsfeld and Prince Sultan bin Abdul Aziz say in news conference in Riyadh that ouster of Hussein creates 'safer region' that allows pullout; photos; only small longstanding training program will continue; US is already pulling out of sprawling air base for new base in Qatar; prince denies asking US to withdraw, but announcement is broadcast on television; Saudis have suggested departure will help them institute political reforms (M)

2003-04-30.title: To the Top of the World, and Down Again
2003-04-30.abstract: Alan Cowell Letter from Europe on fiftieth anniversary of conquest of Mount Everest by Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay; photo; triumph, coming as London prepared for Queen Elizabeth II's coronation, brought flush of pride to Britain, recapturing glory of empire for moment and brightening dreary years after World War II (M)

2003-04-30.title: U.S. Planning to Regroup Armed Forces in Baghdad, Adding to Military Police
2003-04-30.abstract: US will send up to 4,000 soldiers into Baghdad in next few weeks, bringing in more military police and light forces to carry out patrols and improve security; Army units now there often have only tanks and generally sparse US presence makes it difficult to restore order (M)
